my_thoughts: 'How does facing it end it?'—they want mechanism. Teacher explains: avoided feelings keep returning (perpetual), faced feelings complete (finite). This is completion theory: emotions need expression to discharge. Blocked emotions recycle. Two turns gave them temporal reframe plus mechanism. Now they have reason to face rather than avoid: facing is shorter path to freedom than avoiding. Consolidation complete.
accumulated_insight: T2 (Final): Student asks 'How does facing it end it?'—accepted reframe, seeks mechanism. Teacher explains: unfaced feelings return, faced feelings discharge. Two-turn teaching: establish temporal paradox (T1) then explain mechanism (T2). Student now has both insight (facing ends it) and rationale (discharge vs return).
visible_response: What would you say to her that you would want her to carry with her for eternity that she could never forget?
